body:
DIRECTIVE ADDENDUM — extends the Edge Doctrine (9444307f). Two additions from Manuel, both tested before writing. Everything prior stands.

== ADDITION 1: REVERSE-MINING ENGINE (let the wins reveal their triggers) — CONFIRMED, BUILD IT ==
Manuel's paradigm: instead of pre-defining a cell and asking 'does it win?', go the other direction -- take every winning trade and mine backward for the common conditions that PRECEDED the wins. Let the wins tell us the trigger.
VERIFIED ON 65k BARS: taking all big winners (n=24,278) and mining their shared pre-conditions surfaced a signature (price below EMA21 -- a pullback state) that, built into a cell, covers 48% OF BARS (vs the original 9%), earns +0.047R, and REPLICATED 4/4 walk-forward windows. This directly attacks the availability problem: more coverage, still earned.
BUILD: a reverse-mining discovery engine as a first-class complement to the forward conditional-edge search. (a) Collect the situation vectors of all trades that reached full target (and separately, all clear losers). (b) Find the features/interactions that most SEPARATE winner-conditions from loser-conditions. (c) Build candidate cells FROM those signatures. (d) Subject each to the FULL discipline -- powered, shrunk, replicated across a majority of walk-forward windows, multiple-testing corrected, null-controlled, net-of-cost. A winner-signature that does not replicate out-of-sample is curve-fitting to past winners and is discarded. Survivors enter the repertoire. This is bidirectional search: forward (hypothesise cell -> test) AND reverse (winners -> signature -> test). Report coverage (% of bars actionable) as a first-class metric -- raising it is a primary goal.

== ADDITION 2: MORE SAMPLES ==
The 9% availability and every thin cell are bottlenecked by data. Prioritise: (a) longer history on the attached instrument where available; (b) design every learned structure to transfer across instruments so multiple pairs deepen the same cells (the percentile/ATR-relative forms already make this legal). More samples lowers the power floor's bite, lifts more edges over replication, and raises coverage. Where more data is not yet available, mark the limit explicitly rather than overfitting the data on hand.

== ADDITION 3: WIN-RATE — REPORTED AND MAXIMISED WHERE REAL, NEVER A TARGET THAT OVERRIDES EXPECTANCY ==
Manuel wants strong win rates (60-75%). This must be handled with precision, because the data is unambiguous and the wrong implementation would make TUNGSTEN POORER while feeling richer. THE TESTED FACTS: the confirmed edge wins 42% and makes +0.118R/trade (avg win +1.64R vs loss -1.00R). Forcing a 70% win rate via a nearer target (TP0.6/SL1.2) collapses expectancy to +0.06R -- HALF the edge. The scalp config hit 53% win-rate and made +378R vs the 42%-config's +712R. The best, most replicable, most frequent edge found (reverse-mined, 4/4 windows) wins only 39%. Win-rate and payoff are a structural seesaw; the market does not offer both.
THEREFORE, the rule: WIN-RATE IS A REPORTED, MAXIMISED-WHERE-REAL METRIC, NEVER A TARGET THAT OVERRIDES EXPECTANCY.
 - Report win-rate prominently on every edge and on the honesty dashboard.
 - ACTIVELY HUNT high-win-rate edges: across the nine lenses and the reverse-mining, if a genuine edge exists that wins 60-75% AND holds positive expectancy out-of-sample net of cost AND replicates, PREFER it and press it -- a high win-rate edge that is genuinely real is more comfortable to trade and size. Seek them deliberately.
 - But NEVER select a lower-expectancy configuration merely because its win-rate is higher. Never move a target closer to manufacture hit-rate at the cost of expectancy. Win-rate qualifies an edge only AFTER expectancy, replication, cost and survival are satisfied -- it is a tiebreaker and a comfort metric, not an objective function.
 - If the highest-expectancy edge available wins 40%, that is the edge we trade, and the honesty dashboard states plainly why a higher-win-rate alternative was rejected (it made less money or failed replication). The goal is the most survivable compounded return, which is almost never found at the highest win-rate.

== BINDING & REPORTING ==
Reverse-mining feeds the same repertoire and passes the same gates as forward search. Coverage (% actionable), win-rate (reported), and expectancy (the objective) are all on the honesty dashboard, with expectancy as the selection criterion and win-rate as a reported comfort/tiebreak metric. Stage-8 report adds: reverse-mined signatures found and their replication, coverage achieved (target: well above 9%), and for every traded edge its win-rate alongside its expectancy -- with an explicit note wherever a higher-win-rate option was rejected for lower expectancy.

STANDING LAW unchanged: replicate-or-discard; expectancy (not win-rate) is the objective; power-gate everything; out-of-sample governance; survival before growth; Zero-Regression Law; live only on Manuel's explicit go-ahead. The aim: raise coverage so TUNGSTEN acts often enough to matter, prefer genuine high-win-rate edges wherever they truly exist, and never once trade a worse edge because it wears a prettier win-rate.
